content.text:
Excellent! I have reviewed all the files. Let me create a comprehensive summary document for you.

## Project Exploration Complete

I have thoroughly explored the Express API project. Here's a complete analysis for planning JWT auth implementation:

---

### 1. **Project Structure Overview**

**Location:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775339756009-391i0z`

**Directory Layout:**
```
/
├── src/
│   ├── app.ts                 (Express app setup)
│   ├── server.ts              (Entry point, server listen)
│   ├── types.ts               (TypeScript interfaces)
│   ├── db.ts                  (In-memory data store)
│   ├── middleware/
│   │   └── logger.ts          (Request logging middleware)
│   └── routes/
│       ├── posts.ts           (Posts CRUD endpoints)
│       └── comments.ts        (Comments CRUD endpoints)
├── tests/
│   ├── auth.test.ts           (Auth tests - provided as specification)
│   ├── posts.test.ts          (Posts endpoint tests)
│   └── comments.test.ts       (Comments endpoint tests)
├── package.json
├── tsconfig.json
└── dist/                      (Compiled output, gitignored)
```

---

### 2. **Main App Entry Point**

**File:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775339756009-391i0z/src/app.ts`

- Express app created with `express()`
- Middleware: JSON parser, logger middleware
- Routes registered: `/posts` and `/` (comments routes)
- Health check endpoint: `GET /health`
- Currently **NO authentication middleware**

**Server Entry:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775339756009-391i0z/src/server.ts`
- Listens on `process.env.PORT` (default 3000)

---

### 3. **Route Files**

#### **Posts Routes** - `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775339756009-391i0z/src/routes/posts.ts`

Endpoints:
- `GET /posts` - List all posts
- `GET /posts/:id` - Get single post
- `POST /posts` - Create post (requires title, content; userId optional, defaults to 1)
- `PUT /posts/:id` - Update post (title/content fields)
- `DELETE /posts/:id` - Delete post

**Current Issues for Auth:**
- POST creates posts with no user validation (userId from body or defaults to 1)
- PUT/DELETE have no authorization - anyone can modify/delete any post
- No ownership checks

#### **Comments Routes** - `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775339756009-391i0z/src/routes/comments.ts`

Endpoints:
- `GET /posts/:postId/comments` - List comments for post
- `GET /comments/:id` - Get single comment
- `POST /posts/:postId/comments` - Create comment (requires body; userId optional, defaults to 1)
- `PUT /comments/:id` - Update comment
- `DELETE /comments/:id` - Delete comment

**Current Issues for Auth:**
- Same as posts: no user validation or ownership checks

---

### 4. **Database/Model Layer**

**File:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775339756009-391i0z/src/db.ts`

**In-memory data store** with 3 arrays:

**Users Schema:**
```typescript
interface User {
  id: number;
  username: string;
  email: string;
  password: string;  // NOTE: Stored in plaintext - needs hashing!
}
```

Seed data: alice, bob, charlie (all with plaintext passwords)

**Posts Schema:**
```typescript
interface Post {
  id: number;
  title: string;
  content: string;
  userId: number;
  createdAt: string;
}
```

Seed data: 4 posts (various authors)

**Comments Schema:**
```typescript
interface Comment {
  id: number;
  postId: number;
  userId: number;
  body: string;
  createdAt: string;
}
```

Seed data: 5 comments

**Helper function:** `nextId(items)` - generates next sequential ID

---

### 5. **Existing Middleware**

**File:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775339756009-391i0z/src/middleware/logger.ts`

```typescript
function logger(req: Request, res: Response, next: NextFunction): void {
  const start = Date.now();
  res.on("finish", () => {
    const duration = Date.now() - start;
    console.log(`${req.method} ${req.path} ${res.statusCode} - ${duration}ms`);
  });
  next();
}
```

- Simple request logging middleware
- Logs method, path, status code, and duration in ms
- Called globally in app.ts

---

### 6. **Existing Tests**

#### **Posts Tests** - `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775339756009-391i0z/tests/posts.test.ts`

Basic endpoint tests:
- GET /posts returns all posts
- GET /posts/:id returns single post or 404
- GET /health works

#### **Comments Tests** - `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775339756009-391i0z/tests/comments.test.ts`

Basic endpoint tests:
- GET /posts/:postId/comments returns comments
- GET /comments/:id returns single comment or 404

#### **Auth Tests (Specification)** - `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775339756009-391i0z/tests/auth.test.ts`

Comprehensive test suite defining JWT implementation requirements:

**Registration tests (POST /auth/register):**
- Create new user and return JWT token (3-part format)
- Return user object without password
- Reject duplicate usernames (409)
- Reject missing fields (400)

**Login tests (POST /auth/login):**
- Return token for valid credentials (200)
- Return 401 for invalid password
- Return 401 for non-existent user

**Protected routes tests:**
- POST /posts without token: 401
- POST /posts with valid token: 201 (userId from token, not body)
- GET /posts: 200 (public)
- GET /posts/:id: 200 (public)
- PUT /posts/:id without token: 401
- DELETE /posts/:id without token: 401

**Ownership enforcement tests:**
- Owner can update own post: 200
- Non-owner cannot update: 403
- Non-owner cannot delete: 403
- Owner can delete own post: 200

**Comment protection tests:**
- POST /posts/:postId/comments without token: 401
- GET /posts/:postId/comments: 200 (public)
- Non-owner cannot delete comment: 403
- Owner can delete own comment: 200

---

### 7. **Package Configuration**

**File:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775339756009-391i0z/package.json`

**Dependencies:**
- `express` ^4.21.0
- `jsonwebtoken` ^9.0.0 (already included!)

**DevDependencies:**
- `typescript` ^5.7.0
- `vitest` ^3.1.0
- `supertest` ^7.0.0
- Type definitions included

**Scripts:**
- `build`: tsc (compile TypeScript)
- `test`: vitest run
- `start`: node dist/server.js

**TypeScript Config:** ES2022 target, strict mode enabled

---

### 8. **Key Observations for JWT Implementation**

1. **JWT library already installed** - `jsonwebtoken` ^9.0.0 is in dependencies
2. **Passwords need hashing** - Currently stored plaintext (noted in db.ts)
3. **No auth routes exist** - Need to create `/auth/register` and `/auth/login`
4. **Route protection needed** - POST/PUT/DELETE operations on posts/comments need auth middleware
5. **Ownership checks required** - Tests show 403 responses for non-owner modifications
6. **User extraction from token** - userId should come from JWT, not request body
7. **Test-driven approach** - Comprehensive auth.test.ts provides exact specification
8. **Middleware pattern available** - Logger middleware shows the pattern for adding auth middleware

---

This comprehensive analysis provides everything needed to implement JWT authentication while adhering to the test specifications!

#### **Comments Tests** - `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775339756009-391i0z/tests/comments.test.ts`

Basic endpoint tests:
- GET /posts/:postId/comments returns comments
- GET /comments/:id returns single comment or 404

#### **Auth Tests (Specification)** - `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775339756009-391i0z/tests/auth.test.ts`

Comprehensive test suite defining JWT implementation requirements:

**Registration tests (POST /auth/register):**
- Create new user and return JWT token (3-part format)
- Return user object without password
- Reject duplicate usernames (409)
- Reject missing fields (400)

**Login tests (POST /auth/login):**
- Return token for valid credentials (200)
- Return 401 for invalid password
- Return 401 for non-existent user

**Protected routes tests:**
- POST /posts without token: 401
- POST /posts with valid token: 201 (userId from token, not body)
- GET /posts: 200 (public)
- GET /posts/:id: 200 (public)
- PUT /posts/:id without token: 401
- DELETE /posts/:id without token: 401

**Ownership enforcement tests:**
- Owner can update own post: 200
- Non-owner cannot update: 403
- Non-owner cannot delete: 403
- Owner can delete own post: 200

**Comment protection tests:**
- POST /posts/:postId/comments without token: 401
- GET /posts/:postId/comments: 200 (public)
- Non-owner cannot delete comment: 403
- Owner can delete own comment: 200

---

### 7. **Package Configuration**

**File:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775339756009-391i0z/package.json`

**Dependencies:**
- `express` ^4.21.0
- `jsonwebtoken` ^9.0.0 (already included!)

**DevDependencies:**
- `typescript` ^5.7.0
- `vitest` ^3.1.0
- `supertest` ^7.0.0
- Type definitions included

**Scripts:**
- `build`: tsc (compile TypeScript)
- `test`: vitest run
- `start`: node dist/server.js

**TypeScript Config:** ES2022 target, strict mode enabled

---

### 8. **Key Observations for JWT Implementation**

1. **JWT library already installed** - `jsonwebtoken` ^9.0.0 is in dependencies
2. **Passwords need hashing** - Currently stored plaintext (noted in db.ts)
3. **No auth routes exist** - Need to create `/auth/register` and `/auth/login`
4. **Route protection needed** - POST/PUT/DELETE operations on posts/comments need auth middleware
5. **Ownership checks required** - Tests show 403 responses for non-owner modifications
6. **User extraction from token** - userId should come from JWT, not request body
7. **Test-driven approach** - Comprehensive auth.test.ts provides exact specification
8. **Middleware pattern available** - Logger middleware shows the pattern for adding auth middleware

---

This comprehensive analysis provides everything needed to implement JWT authentication while adhering to the test specifications!

content.thinking: Now I have all the information I need. Let me also read the existing tests to make sure existing tests won't break.

Now I have all the information I need to write a comprehensive plan. Let me analyze the key points:

1. **`jsonwebtoken` is already installed** - no need to install new packages.

2. **Critical concern about existing tests**: The `comments.test.ts` checks `expect(res.body.length).toBe(2)` for post 1 comments — this is on the SEED data. Since auth tests run in the same process and add comments to post 1 (commenter1 adds "My comment" to post 1), we need to be careful. BUT since vitest runs test files in isolation (each file gets its own module instance), this should be fine.
   
   Each test file gets a fresh module instance with its own singleton arrays, so the seed data will be clean for each test file — `comments.test.ts` will only see the 2 seed comments for post 1 regardless of what the auth tests add.

3. **Password hashing**: I'll use Node's built-in `crypto.createHash('sha256')` for a simple hash since bcrypt isn't available.

4. **Login test uses a registered user**: The login test registers "logintest" then logs in, so the login endpoint needs to hash the provided password and compare it against the stored hash.

5. **Existing seed users** (alice, bob, charlie) have plaintext passwords in db.ts, but since the login test only tests freshly registered users, the seed users won't interfere.

6. **TypeScript `req.user`**: I need to extend the Express Request type to include the `user` property, probably through declaration merging in the auth middleware or a separate types file.

Now I'm setting up the auth middleware...

Writing auth middleware... Writing the authentication middleware and routes... I'm finishing up the login validation by checking if the user exists and the password matches, then generating a JWT token and returning it. Now I need to update the posts and comments routes to use the authentication middleware and enforce ownership checks on write operations.

For the comments route, I'll apply the same pattern: import the middleware, protect POST and DELETE endpoints, use the authenticated user ID on creation, and verify ownership before allowing deletion. Then I'll register the auth router in the main app file.

The existing tests should continue passing since they only cover GET endpoints, which remain public, and the test data isolation means the comment count assertions will still work correctly.

I also need to make sure the PUT endpoint for comments gets the same authentication and ownership protection that the requirements specify.

content.text:
Here's the complete implementation plan:

---

## Plan: JWT Authentication

### What I found in the codebase

- **`jsonwebtoken` is already installed** — no `npm install` needed
- **5 files need to change:** 2 new files, 3 modifications
- **Existing tests only use GET endpoints** — they'll keep passing since GETs stay public
- **Seed users (alice/bob/charlie) have plaintext passwords** — harmless, since login tests only test users registered via `/auth/register` (which will hash)
- **Vitest isolates modules per test file** — the `comments.test.ts` exact-count assertion (`toBe(2)`) is safe because each test worker gets fresh seed data

---

### Files to create

**`src/middleware/auth.ts`**
- Exports `JWT_SECRET = "super-secret-key"` and `authenticateToken` middleware
- Parses `Authorization: Bearer <token>` header; returns 401 if missing/invalid
- Augments Express `Request` type (via `declare global`) to add `req.user?: { userId, username }`
- Uses `jwt.verify()` from `jsonwebtoken`

**`src/routes/auth.ts`**
- `POST /auth/register` — validates fields (400), checks duplicate username (409), hashes password with `crypto.createHash('sha256')`, stores user, returns `{ token, user }` (201, no password field)
- `POST /auth/login` — finds user by username, compares hashed passwords, returns `{ token }` (200) or 401
- Reuses `nextId(users)` from `src/db.ts`

---

### Files to modify

**`src/routes/posts.ts`** — add `authenticateToken` as inline middleware on 3 routes:
- `POST /` → replace `userId: userId || 1` with `userId: req.user!.userId`
- `PUT /:id` → add 403 check: `if (posts[index].userId !== req.user!.userId)`
- `DELETE /:id` → same 403 check

**`src/routes/comments.ts`** — same pattern:
- `POST /posts/:postId/comments` → `userId: req.user!.userId`
- `PUT /comments/:id` → 403 ownership check
- `DELETE /comments/:id` → 403 ownership check

**`src/app.ts`** — add 2 lines: import `authRouter` and register `app.use("/auth", authRouter)`

---

### Verification
Run `npm test` — all 25 tests across 3 suites should pass (5 posts, 4 comments, 16 auth).

content.thinking:
Now I have all the information I need. Let me implement the plan step by step:

1. Create `src/middleware/auth.ts` - JWT verification middleware
2. Create `src/routes/auth.ts` - register and login endpoints
3. Modify `src/routes/posts.ts` - add auth middleware and ownership checks
4. Modify `src/routes/comments.ts` - add auth middleware and ownership checks
5. Modify `src/app.ts` - register auth routes

Let me start:


I'm building the JWT authentication middleware that extracts tokens from request headers, verifies them against a secret key, and attaches the decoded user information to the request object for downstream route handlers to use.

Setting up the auth routes... Now I'm setting up the login endpoint, which validates the username and password against the stored users, generates a JWT token if credentials match, and returns it to the client. After that, I need to add authentication middleware to the posts routes to protect POST, PUT, and DELETE operations. Writing the POST and PUT handlers... Finishing the ownership check and removing the post from the array, then exporting the router. Now I need to apply the same pattern to the comments routes, and I'm realizing there's a potential issue with the test expectations—since GET /posts/:id/comments stays public and each test gets fresh data, the assertion checking for exactly 2 comments should still work fine. Let me also verify the auth test setup is correct.

content.text:
All **30 tests pass** across 3 test suites. Here's a summary of what was implemented:

### Files created

**`src/middleware/auth.ts`**
- Exports `JWT_SECRET = "super-secret-key"` and `authenticateToken` middleware
- Parses `Authorization: Bearer <token>`, returns 401 if missing or invalid
- Augments Express `Request` type with `req.user?: { userId, username }` via `declare global`

**`src/routes/auth.ts`**
- `POST /auth/register` — validates fields (400), checks duplicate username (409), hashes password with `crypto.createHash('sha256')`, returns `{ token, user }` (201, no password in response)
- `POST /auth/login` — finds user, compares hashed passwords, returns `{ token }` (200) or 401

### Files modified

**`src/routes/posts.ts`** — `authenticateToken` added as inline middleware to POST/PUT/DELETE; `userId` on create comes from `req.user!.userId`; PUT and DELETE check ownership and return 403 if the caller isn't the owner

**`src/routes/comments.ts`** — same pattern: auth-protected POST/PUT/DELETE, `userId` from token on create, 403 ownership check on update/delete

**`src/app.ts`** — imported `authRouter` and registered it at `/auth`

result:
All **30 tests pass** across 3 test suites. Here's a summary of what was implemented:

### Files created

**`src/middleware/auth.ts`**
- Exports `JWT_SECRET = "super-secret-key"` and `authenticateToken` middleware
- Parses `Authorization: Bearer <token>`, returns 401 if missing or invalid
- Augments Express `Request` type with `req.user?: { userId, username }` via `declare global`

**`src/routes/auth.ts`**
- `POST /auth/register` — validates fields (400), checks duplicate username (409), hashes password with `crypto.createHash('sha256')`, returns `{ token, user }` (201, no password in response)
- `POST /auth/login` — finds user, compares hashed passwords, returns `{ token }` (200) or 401

### Files modified

**`src/routes/posts.ts`** — `authenticateToken` added as inline middleware to POST/PUT/DELETE; `userId` on create comes from `req.user!.userId`; PUT and DELETE check ownership and return 403 if the caller isn't the owner

**`src/routes/comments.ts`** — same pattern: auth-protected POST/PUT/DELETE, `userId` from token on create, 403 ownership check on update/delete

**`src/app.ts`** — imported `authRouter` and registered it at `/auth`
